figures.caption: Figure 1: An example of a task instance from the RACE-bench. During agent inference, only the Feature Request and FTP tests are provided as the observable inputs, while the Environment Setup specifies the execution context for evaluation. Each instance additionally includes structured Reasoning Ground Truth, the corresponding Gold Patch, and Unit Tests, enabling end-to-end evaluation of both intermediate reasoning processes and final patch correctness.

Table 3: Reasoning evaluation results on RACE-bench (mean scores). “Success”/“Failure” are split by patch success. We additionally report “Failure (excl. apply-fail)” whose patches apply successfully but fail at least one test.

Table 4: Patch-level and reasoning-level results on RACE-bench Lite under different base LLMs. We report the intermediate reasoning metrics for the Failure (excl. apply-fail) group. Here, R@ and O@ denote Recall and OverPrediction, respectively.
Figure 3: Average conversation messages for interactive agents under different base LLMs on RACE-bench Lite.
